sections.paragraph:
with suff. נִסִּי (from the root נָסַס No. II) something lifted up, a token to be seen far off, specially-

(1) a banner, such as was set up on high mountains, especially in case of an invasion, when it shewed the people where to assemble, Isa 5:26 11:12 18:3 62:10 Jerem. 4:6 , 21 Psa 60:6. Compare מַשְׂאֵת. No. 2.

(2) a standard, or flag, as of a ship, Eze 27:7 Isa 33:23.

(3) a column or lofty pole, Num 21:8, Num 21:9.

(4) metaphorically, a sign, by which any one is warned, Num 26:10. (Syr. ܢܚܺܫܐܳ a sign, a banner).
